INDIA’S TEXTILE EXPORTERS GAIN EDGE AS UK, EU TRADE DEALS BOOST COMPETITIVENESS: NIKKEI 

India’s textile and apparel exporters are gaining momentum as trade agreements with the U.K. and progress toward an EU trade deal improve market access.

Lower tariffs and preferential access are expected to help Indian exporters compete more effectively against Bangladesh and Pakistan, which have traditionally enjoyed tariff advantages in key export markets.

The agreements could increase export orders, improve capacity utilization, and support long-term growth for India’s textile sector.

**JSW Steel | Q1FY27 Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- Adjusted EBITDA/ton **₹14,990**.
- Up from **₹12,232 QoQ**.
- Operating profitability improved.
- Earnings remained strong.

**Balance Sheet**
- Net debt **₹46,157 crore**.
- Down from **₹53,870 crore**.
- Leverage reduced significantly.
- Balance sheet strengthened.

**Overseas Operations**
- US EBITDA **US$15.9 million**.
- Italy EBITDA **€7.1 million**.
- Overseas business turned profitable.
- Operational performance improved.

**Brokerage Views**
- **Jefferies:** Buy, TP **₹1,650**.
- **Motilal Oswal:** Buy, TP **₹1,470**.
- **Nuvama:** Reduce, TP **₹1,172**.
- Consensus remains constructive.

**Growth Outlook**
- Capacity target **80 MT** by FY32.
- EPS expected to treble FY26–FY29.
- Margin outlook remains healthy.
- Long-term expansion continues.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JSW Steel delivered a strong quarter with higher EBITDA per tonne, sharp debt reduction and a turnaround in overseas operations. While brokerages differ on valuation, most remain positive on the company's long-term earnings growth, capacity expansion and improving profitability.**

ZEEL: REDEEMS USD 23.9 MILLION FCCBs 💰📉

• 💵 Zee Entertainment has redeemed outstanding FCCBs worth USD 23.90 million, along with accrued interest

• 🏦 Redemption has been completed in accordance with the terms of the FCCBs

AMARA RAJA: APPCB REVOKES CLOSURE ORDERS FOR MANUFACTURING UNITS ✅🏭

• 🏭 Andhra Pradesh Pollution Control Board (APPCB) has revoked the closure orders for Amara Raja's manufacturing facilities in Tirupati and Chittoor districts

• 📜 Revocation pertains to closure orders originally issued in 2021, with subsequent updates in 2023

• ▶️ Manufacturing facilities can continue/restore operations following the revocation

• 💰 Company stated there is no financial impact arising from this development

• 🌱 Reiterated its commitment to maintaining high environmental, health and safety standards

🟢 Impact: Positive (Removes a longstanding regulatory overhang and supports uninterrupted manufacturing operations.)

ALEMBIC PHARMA: PARTNER NATCO GETS USFDA TENTATIVE NOD FOR OLAPARIB TABLETS 🇺🇸💊

• ✅ Partner NATCO Pharma received USFDA tentative approval for Olaparib Tablets (100 mg & 150 mg)

• 🤝 Alembic will market and distribute the product in the U.S., while NATCO will manufacture it

• 💊 Generic version references AstraZeneca's Lynparza, used for approved oncology indications

• 📊 U.S. market size for Olaparib Tablets is estimated at ~US$1.4 billion (IQVIA, MAT March 2026)

• ⚖️ Para IV litigation is ongoing, with commercial launch subject to litigation outcome

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens Alembic's U.S. oncology pipeline and provides a significant future revenue opportunity, though launch timing depends on litigation.)

ICICI Bank – Q1FY27 FC: Firing on all cylinders

# ICICI Bank reported a 10% beat on PAT/RoA at 2.5% at INR148bn driven by healthy NII growth, and higher other income, partly offset by higher staff expenses and provisions.

# Credit growth was robust and system beating at 19.6% YoY/ 5% QoQ, mainly driven by corporate, rural and business banking, but retail growth remains relatively slow at 12% yoy. 

# NIM improved 4bps QoQ to 4.36% in Q1 while the peers have reported decline in margins. This could be partly due to better int income on RBI balances and lower cash on book. 

# Asset quality remained largely stable with GNPA ratio declining 2bps QoQ at 1.38%. Gross slippages stood lower at INR55.5bn in Q1FY27 versus our expectations of INR60.4bn.

# The bank holds contingency provision of INR131bn, and additional standard asset provision of INR12.8bn made in Q3 2026 as directed by RBI in respect of the agricultural priority sector portfolio.

ICICI has posted an all around strong performance delivering robust growth, margins and profitability. Broker Note

Q1FY27 Results – Punjab National Bank
Rev. growth: 3.1% YoY & 2.4% QoQ
OP margins: 8.2% in Q1FY27 vs 4.7% in Q1FY26
PAT growth: 191.4% YoY & 2.2% QoQ

Result Highlights:
- PAT surged 191.4% YoY primarily on a sharply lower income tax provision of ₹1,725 Cr versus ₹5,083 Cr in Q1FY26, when the bank had booked a one-time tax charge from its transition to the new tax regime - a base effect rather than a core profitability jump of similar scale.
- Gross NPA improved to 2.78% from 3.78% a year earlier, reducing provisioning needs and supporting higher operating profit even as core interest income growth stayed modest - NII rose just 2.1% YoY to ₹10,798 Cr, with NIM improving only slightly QoQ to 2.50% from 2.47%.
- Credit mix continued shifting toward Retail, Agri and MSME segments, now over 54% of domestic advances, while capital adequacy strengthened to 18.13% CRAR from 17.50% YoY; the board separately approved a ₹7,500 Cr fundraise via QIP/FPO to support future balance sheet growth.

Management Commentary:
- The bank said core interest income growth remained moderate even as it continued to strengthen its balance sheet.
- Credit focus has shifted toward Retail, Agri and MSME (RAM) segments, now over 54% of domestic advances.
Sarda Energy board to meet on 1st Aug for Result and Fundraise.

TATA POWER: SECI AWARDS 324 MW PUMPED STORAGE PROJECT ⚡💧

• 🏆 Received a Letter of Award (LoA) from SECI for providing energy storage from a 324 MW / 2,592 MWh Pumped Storage Plant (PSP)

• 📅 Project awarded for a 40-year storage service period

• ⚡ Supply of storage capacity to commence within 36 months of the effective PPA date

• 💰 Annual fixed charge of ₹1.084 crore per MW, translating to ~₹351.3 crore annual fixed revenue for the 324 MW capacity

• 🤝 SECI will sign the Pumped Storage Purchase Agreement (PPA) after execution of the Pumped Storage Sale Agreement

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens Tata Power's long-term renewable energy storage portfolio with stable, annuity-like revenue visibility.)
'Just became a grandfather!' - Outgoing Kotak Bank CEO Ashok Vaswani explains the reason behind his exit

Skyroot Aerospace has become the first private company outside the United States and China to successfully place payloads into orbit, making India only the third country where a private company has achieved an orbital launch.

That's the true significance of today's launch. It marks India's transition from being a spacefaring nation led by ISRO to one where private companies can also compete at the highest level.

India generates approximately 72,000 MLD of sewage daily. Installed STP capacity is barely 26,000 MLD. Even of that, barely 60–70% operates reliably. The CPCB has mandated timelines for all Class-I and Class-II cities. This single statistic represents a ₹2+ lakh crore opportunity for the next decade.

Indegene Annual Report Notes  

1) AI in life sciences is not a horizontal technology problem. It is a deeply domain-intensive one. Every workflow is regulated. Every decision must be auditable. Every output is accountable to a patient. Generic foundation models cannot reason about an FDA labeling guideline, a Phase III endpoint, a payer dossier, or the semantics of a medico-legal review. What is required is a life sciences-native operating system - fusing 27 years of regulatory, therapeutic and commercial depth with a proprietary intelligent data layer and a fabric of AI agents engineered for the workflows that move molecules from lab to patient.

Cortex - Gen AI platform that enables 27 years of data in domain expertise and data intelligence for HCP from tandem and Invisage ( HCP 3M + digital  ) 

2) revenue models and economics are more outcome based rather than person /hours based net revenue retention above 100% every year for the last 5 years, our top-20 client cohort tripled in revenue

3) Have seen focused industry pivots in the past and this is one we are embracing to provide better solutions to clients 

4) Clients are increasingly looking to have a partner rather than just a vendor , capability additions on recent Europe and Warn Acquisitions help bridge into better capabilities as well

ORBIT ACHIEVED. 🚀 

Vikram-1 Test Flight-1 has reached orbit. India's first privately developed orbital rocket has completed its final burn and injected its payloads into a ~450 km orbit, making India the third country in the world with private orbital launch capability. 

History is made. 🇮🇳

Gujarat Fluorochemicals (GFL) – Plant Visit Note (Jolva Battery Materials Complex | 16 July 2026)

GFL is no longer just a fluorochemicals company. It is positioning itself as one of the world's most integrated battery materials platforms.

Here's what stood out:
Current Business Mix (Q4 FY26)
 - Fluoropolymers: 62%
 - Fluorochemicals: 23%
 - Bulk Chemicals: 12%
 - Battery Materials: 1% (commercial revenue started in FY26 and is now ramping up)

While battery materials contribute very little today, management expects this to become the company's next major growth engine.

Battery Materials – Commercialisation Has Begun

The battery materials business has moved beyond the investment phase.
Key updates:
 - LiPF₆ commercial production commenced in Dec'25.
 - First production line is expected to reach full utilisation by the end of FY27.
 - Phase-1 capacities have largely been commissioned and contracted.
 - Commercial sales are already scaling up with orders secured for FY27 and beyond.

Management is targeting:
 - ₹500–600 crore battery materials revenue in FY27
- EBITDA break-even during FY27
 - 20–25% EBITDA margins by FY28 as utilisation improves.
 
High Entry Barriers
 - This isn't a commodity business.
 - Battery chemical suppliers typically require 18–24 months of customer qualification, progressing through multiple approval stages (A/B/C/D).

Management highlighted:
 - GFL has already cleared C-stage qualification for LiPF₆.
 - Pricing has already been agreed using formula-linked contracts, reducing pricing uncertainty once commercial supplies ramp.
This significantly improves revenue visibility.

Building One of the Most Integrated Battery Platforms
Today GFL manufactures:
 - LiPF₆ electrolyte salt
 - Electrolytes
 - LFP Cathode Materials
 - PVDF Binders
The next addition will be:
 - Natural Graphite Anode Materials

Once operational, management estimates GFL will participate in nearly 70% of the value of an LFP battery cell, making it one of the most integrated battery materials companies globally.

Customer Approvals Continue to Improve
Management disclosed approvals across multiple geographies:
 - Electrolytes approved by 4 Japanese & Korean companies
 - LFP approved by 3 Korean companies and multiple US customers
 - LiPF₆ approvals received from several major global electrolyte manufacturers
This reduces customer concentration risk and supports future capacity utilisation.

Massive Growth Opportunity
Global lithium battery demand (excluding China) is expected to increase from:
~500 GWh in CY26
To ~1.8 TWh by CY30
GFL is positioning itself to participate in this structural growth cycle.

Significant Capex Underway
 - Battery materials remains the company's largest investment area.
 - Investment made so far: ~₹2,000 crore
 - FY27 Battery Materials Capex: ~₹2,300 crore
  - Total planned EV/Battery Materials Capex by FY28: ~₹6,000 crore
Management expects the full earnings potential to emerge in FY29, once utilisation normalises.

Competitive Advantages
 A few interesting observations from the visit:
 - GFL manufactures crystalline LiPF₆ using licensed technology.
 - Establishing a LiPF₆ facility typically requires ~18 months, creating meaningful execution barriers.
 - The company already produces Anhydrous Hydrofluoric Acid (AHF) internally, a critical raw material for battery chemicals.
 - Dedicated AHF capacity for battery materials is also being planned, strengthening backward integration.
 - Fluoropolymers Continue to Drive Earnings
While battery materials are scaling up, the legacy chemicals business continues to perform well.

Management reiterated:
 - FY27 Fluoropolymer growth of 15–20%
- Existing fluoropolymer expansion expected to reach optimal utilisation during FY27
 - New capacity additions focused on high-value specialty fluoropolymers, particularly for semiconductors, EVs, battery storage and clean energy applications.

R-32 Refrigerants
 Another emerging growth driver:
 - Production commenced in March 2026
 - Current capacity: 10,000 tonnes
 - Ramp-up to 20,000 tonnes underway
Demand supported by residential ACs, commercial refrigeration and AI data centre cooling infrastructure.

Regulatory Tailwinds
Management believes evolving US industrial policy under the One Big Beautiful Bill (OBBB) could accelerate localisation of battery chemical sourcing and reduce dependence on China.
According to management, GFL's battery material products are OBBB compliant, potentially positioning the company as an alternative global supplier.

Key Takeaway
The investment case for GFL is gradually shifting.
Historically, it was driven by fluoropolymers and fluorochemicals. Over the next few years, the key variables to watch will be:
 - Battery materials revenue ramp
 - Customer qualification milestones
 - Capacity utilisation
 - Margin expansion
 - Execution of the ₹6,000 crore capex programme

If management executes successfully, GFL could evolve from a specialty fluorochemicals company into a globally integrated battery materials platform with multiple long-term growth drivers.

• 📅 Inspection was carried out from July 13–17, 2026

CHEMPLAST SANMAR: MINOR FIRE AT KARAIKAL EDC PLANT 🔥

• 🔥 Minor fire incident reported at the Ethylene Di-Chloride (EDC) plant in Karaikal, Puducherry on July 17 around 10:30 AM

• ✅ No injuries or casualties reported

• 🔍 Company is assessing the impact on equipment, instruments and electrical installations

• 🛡️ Damage from the incident is adequately covered by insurance

• 🏭 Chemplast is taking necessary steps to restore EDC plant operations at the earliest and will provide further updates

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Slightly Negative (No human impact and insurance coverage limit the financial risk, but production disruption remains a near-term monitorable.)

𝗦𝗧𝗟 | 𝗔 𝗟𝗮𝘂𝗻𝗰𝗵 𝘄𝗶𝘁𝗵 𝗕𝗶𝗴 𝗜𝗺𝗽𝗹𝗶𝗰𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀

Sterilite tech has launched 𝗖𝗢𝗡𝗖𝗔𝗧, a 𝗽𝗿𝗲-𝗰𝗼𝗻𝗻𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗼𝗿𝗶𝘇𝗲𝗱 𝗳𝗶𝗯𝗲𝗿 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝗻𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝘀𝗼𝗹𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, in the US after successful field trials with one of the 𝗹𝗮𝗿𝗴𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗨𝗦 𝘁𝗲𝗹𝗲𝗰𝗼𝗺 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗼𝗿𝘀.

Instead of technicians spending hours 𝗷𝗼𝗶𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 (𝘀𝗽𝗹𝗶𝗰𝗶𝗻𝗴) fiber cables on-site, CONCAT comes 𝗳𝗮𝗰𝘁𝗼𝗿𝘆-𝗮𝘀𝘀𝗲𝗺𝗯𝗹𝗲𝗱 and is ready to install, reducing deployment time while delivering 𝘂𝗽 𝘁𝗼 𝟳𝟭% 𝗹𝗮𝗯𝗼𝗿 𝗰𝗼𝘀𝘁 𝘀𝗮𝘃𝗶𝗻𝗴𝘀.

- STL is gradually moving from selling 𝗳𝗶𝗯𝗲𝗿 𝗰𝗮𝗯𝗹𝗲𝘀 to selling 𝘀𝗼𝗹𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀. 
- If products like CONCAT see wider adoption, the company can potentially 𝗲𝗮𝗿𝗻 𝗺𝗼𝗿𝗲 𝗽𝗲𝗿 𝗳𝗶𝗯𝗲𝗿 𝗱𝗲𝗽𝗹𝗼𝘆𝗲𝗱, supporting a better 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗱𝘂𝗰𝘁 𝗺𝗶𝘅 and 𝗺𝗮𝗿𝗴𝗶𝗻𝘀 over time.

Clarification issued by Acme Solar Holdings Ltd

These fire claims are factually incorrect.

A localized fire caused by an IGBT/short-circuit issue affected only four Power Conversion Systems (PCS). No BESS containers were damaged. The incident was promptly brought under control by our in-house fire safety team in accordance with established safety protocols. The estimated revenue impact is not material and is approximately INR 20 lakh. The affected equipment is being restored using available spares, with completion expected within a week.

**Kusumgar | Business & Valuation View**

**Business Profile**
- Specialized technical fabrics manufacturer.
- Around **50%** revenue from aerospace & defence.
- Operates in a niche segment.
- No listed domestic peer.

**Brokerage View**
- Emkay has initiated coverage.
- Target price: **₹800**.
- Valuation benchmarked to aerospace & defence peers.

**Valuation Perspective**
- Business remains primarily a textile manufacturer.
- Aerospace exposure enhances growth prospects.
- Premium valuation driven by niche positioning.
- Current valuation leaves limited margin of safety.

**Investment Considerations**
- IPO price of **₹419** offered reasonable valuation.
- Current price around **₹580** appears more demanding.
- Small-cap bull market could support premium multiples.
- Lack of listed peers may sustain valuation premium.

**Key Risks**
- Elevated valuation risk.
- Multiple contraction if sentiment weakens.
- Execution of growth plans remains critical.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Kusumgar offers a unique niche play in specialized technical fabrics with significant exposure to the aerospace & defence sector. While its differentiated business may justify a valuation premium, the sharp post-listing rally has reduced the margin of safety, making future returns increasingly dependent on sustained earnings execution rather than further valuation expansion.**

• 📉 The order prohibits them from buying, selling or otherwise dealing in securities, directly or indirectly, and from being associated with the securities market during the restraint period

• 🏢 Mauria Udyog stated that the order has no material impact on its financials, operations or other business activities

LASER POWER & INFRA: BAGS FIRST ₹4.15 CR HTLS CONDUCTOR ORDER FROM HPSEBL ⚡🏗️

• 🏆 Laser Power & Infra has received its first Letter of Intent (LoI) from Himachal Pradesh State Electricity Board Ltd. (HPSEBL) for a turnkey reconductoring project involving the supply and installation of High Temperature Low Sag (HTLS) conductors in the Nalagarh Electrical Division, valued at approximately ₹4.15 crore 

• 🇺🇸⚡ The project marks the company's first commercial deployment under its TS Conductor (USA) manufacturing partnership, using Aluminium Encapsulated Carbon Core Conductors (AECC) manufactured in India with TS Conductor's proprietary carbon fibre composite core technology 

• 🚆 The company said this is possibly India's first commercial project using TS Conductor's proprietary AECC technology, representing a key milestone in localising next-generation transmission conductor technology 

• 📈 AECC conductors can deliver 2–3 times the power carrying capacity of conventional ACSR conductors while reducing line losses and enabling grid upgrades without replacing existing transmission towers 

• 📋 Laser Power has already pre-qualified and submitted bids for 18 transmission EPC projects involving HTLS reconductoring, with this HPSEBL order providing its first commercial reference in the segment 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The first commercial AECC order validates Laser Power's advanced conductor technology strategy, strengthens its position in India's grid modernisation market and opens opportunities for future HTLS projects.)

• ✍️ PNC Infratech has signed Concession Agreements with the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) for two Hybrid Annuity Mode (HAM) highway projects in Uttar Pradesh with a total bid project cost of ₹3,483 crore 

• 🛣️ Package-I covers the 4-laning of the Barabanki–Mustafabad section of NH-927 through SPV Barabanki Mustafabad Highway Pvt. Ltd., with a bid project cost of ₹1,728 crore 

• 🚧 Package-II covers the 4-laning of the Mustafabad–Biswariya section of NH-927 through SPV Mustafabad Biswariya Highway Pvt. Ltd., with a bid project cost of ₹1,755 crore 

• ⏳ Both projects are to be constructed within 24 months from the respective appointed dates and will be operated for 15 years after construction under the HAM model

TIME TECHNOPLAST: BAGS ₹38.14 CR HPCL ORDER FOR 1.4 LAKH COMPOSITE LPG CYLINDERS 🛢️📦

• 🏆 Time Technoplast has secured a Government e-Marketplace (GeM) contract from Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Ltd. (HPCL) for the supply of 1,40,000 units of 10 kg Type IV Composite LPG Cylinders valued at approximately ₹38.14 crore 

• 🛢️ The order will be executed within six months and supports the growing adoption of lightweight, safer composite LPG cylinders by India's leading oil marketing companies 

• ⚡ The company highlighted that HPCL has introduced composite LPG cylinder distribution through leading quick-commerce platforms, enabling on-demand delivery and improving consumer convenience 

• 📈 Management said the order reinforces Time Technoplast's leadership in the composite cylinder business and supports India's transition towards modern, customer-centric LPG distribution 

• ✅ The order is from a domestic entity, with no promoter interest and no related-party transaction involved 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The sizeable HPCL order strengthens Time Technoplast's order book, reinforces its leadership in composite LPG cylinders and supports long-term growth in the clean energy packaging segment.)

MAX INDIA: ANTARA INTRODUCES ROBOTIC-ASSISTED REHABILITATION AT BENGALURU CARE HOME 🤖🏥

• 🤖 Antara Care Homes, a wholly owned subsidiary of Max India, has introduced ExoAtlet II, a robotic-assisted exoskeleton for rehabilitation at its Bannerghatta, Bengaluru care home 

• 🩺 The new technology strengthens Antara's specialist-led rehabilitation programme for patients recovering from stroke, paralysis, neurological and orthopaedic conditions, as well as functional loss after prolonged illness 

• 🚶 ExoAtlet II is a wearable robotic suit that supports lower-limb movement and enables patients to perform hundreds of consistent, physiologically accurate walking steps during each rehabilitation session 

• 🏥 The Bengaluru centre now offers robotic-assisted gait training alongside robotic hand and finger retraining, delivered through a multidisciplinary team including PM&R specialists, physiotherapists, occupational therapists, speech therapists and psychologists 

• 🌍 Antara currently operates 8 care facilities with 485 beds across Delhi-NCR, Bengaluru and Chennai, providing rehabilitation, transition care and assisted living services for seniors 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The addition of robotic rehabilitation enhances Antara's premium healthcare offering, strengthens its technology-led differentiation and supports long-term growth in senior care and rehabilitation services.)

DE NEERS TOOLS: Q1 FY27 REVENUE JUMPS 28%; DUBAI ARM & POLYCAB ORDER BOOST GROWTH 🛠️📈

• 📈 Consolidated Q1 FY27 revenue rose 27.8% YoY to ₹46.8 crore, while standalone revenue increased 23.9% YoY to ₹44.8 crore (provisional & unaudited) 

• 🌍 De Neers Tools LLC (Dubai subsidiary) reported ₹2.04 crore revenue in Q1 FY27, up sharply from ₹0.50 crore in Q1 FY26 

• 🏆 The company secured an order from Polycab India to supply 35,000 electrician tool kits, strengthening its position in the professional tools segment 

• ⚡ The company continued to witness strong demand for its specialised tool kits, including EV tool kits, reflecting healthy customer traction and long-term relationships 

• 🏭 De Neers has also entered into a 51% joint venture to manufacture tools, bits, sockets and allied products, supporting backward integration and strengthening manufacturing capabilities 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong revenue growth, rising overseas contribution, a large Polycab order and strategic backward integration strengthen De Neers' long-term growth outlook.)

BIKEWO GREEN TECH: PARTNERS WITH YUBHAS RENEWABLES FOR SOLAR-ASSISTED EV PILOT ☀️🛺

• 🤝 BikeWo Green Tech has partnered with Yubhas Renewables to begin operational validation of solar-assisted electric three-wheelers for rural and semi-urban last-mile mobility 

• 🛺 The pilot will evaluate the vehicles under commercial operating conditions, assessing range, battery efficiency, payload capacity, ride quality, thermal performance, braking, operating economics and overall reliability before commercial deployment 

• 🌱 The partnership also aims to promote rural entrepreneurship by training youth in vehicle operations, maintenance, customer service and business management, enabling them to become owner-operators 

• 💰 Eligible participants may also access financing under the Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana, subject to eligibility and lender approvals, supporting adoption of clean mobility solutions in rural India 

• 🚀 The companies aim to build a scalable ecosystem that supports passenger transport, agricultural logistics, village deliveries and healthcare access, while accelerating EV adoption and sustainable transportation 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The partnership strengthens BikeWo's clean mobility ecosystem, supports innovation in solar-assisted EVs and creates long-term opportunities in rural transportation and entrepreneurship.)

LTM: PARTNERS WITH GLEAN TO ACCELERATE ENTERPRISE AI ADOPTION 🤖🤝

• 🤝 LTM has entered into a strategic partnership with Glean to help enterprises accelerate AI adoption by combining LTM's BlueVerse™ agentic AI ecosystem with Glean's enterprise AI platform and knowledge graph 

• 🧠 The partnership aims to solve the challenge of fragmented enterprise knowledge by enabling secure, permission-aware access to information across multiple business applications, systems and workflows 

• 🏦 The joint offering will focus on AI deployment across IT support, enterprise knowledge management and enterprise application operations, especially for banking, financial services, insurance, manufacturing and other highly regulated industries 

• 📈 The collaboration is expected to deliver measurable benefits including faster incident resolution, higher employee productivity, lower support costs, quicker onboarding, enhanced enterprise search and more efficient application support 

• 🚀 Management said the partnership strengthens LTM's AI-led transformation portfolio and enables customers to scale enterprise AI while maintaining governance, security and compliance 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The strategic AI partnership enhances LTM's enterprise AI capabilities, strengthens its digital transformation offerings and supports long-term growth in AI-led services.)

SBI | SBI Funds Management IPO Update

IPO Milestone
- Prospectus filed with ROC, Mumbai.
- Major step towards IPO completion.
- Filed on 16 July 2026.

Offer Structure
- Pure Offer for Sale (OFS).
- SBI selling 9.95 crore shares (4.8851%).
- Amundi selling 7.15 crore shares (3.5082%).
- No fresh equity issuance.

Pricing
- Offer price fixed at ₹574/share.
- Employee discount of ₹54/share.
- Allotment expected around 20 July 2026.

FY26 Financial Highlights
- Total income: ₹4,969.09 crore.
- Reserves & Surplus: ₹3,533.09 crore.

Implications for SBI
- Unlocks value of subsidiary.
- Highlights strength of asset management franchise.
- Potential improvement in capital flexibility.
- Enhances transparency through public listing.

Key Takeaway
- The filing of the final prospectus marks a significant milestone for SBI Funds Management's IPO. The listing is expected to unlock value for SBI shareholders while showcasing the strong financial position and scale of India's largest asset management business.

RBI Governor | Key Highlights

### Economic Outlook
- Indian economy remains strong.
- Policies remain growth-oriented.
- Medium-term outlook remains positive.

### Inflation
- Inflation driven by supply-side factors.
- No major demand-side concerns indicated.

### Financial Stability
- Banking system remains well capitalized.
- Capital adequacy ratio above 17%.
- Statutory liquidity ratio above 120%.
- Financial system remains resilient.

### Credit Growth
- Strong growth in Gold Loans.
- Healthy MSME credit growth.
- RBI sees no immediate risk.

### External Sector
- Balance of Payments expected to remain strong.
- External position remains comfortable.

### Key Risk
- Middle East conflict remains a major concern.
- Geopolitical tensions continue to be monitored.

Market Read Through
- Positive for Banking & Financials.
- Supportive for Gold Loan NBFCs.
- Positive for MSME-focused lenders.
- Macro outlook remains constructive.
- Geopolitical risks warrant monitoring.

Key Takeaway
- The RBI Governor reaffirmed confidence in India's macroeconomic fundamentals, highlighting a strong banking system, healthy credit growth and a resilient external position. While inflation is viewed as largely supply-driven, geopolitical developments in the Middle East remain the key external risk to monitor.

RAJESH POWER SERVICES: BAGS ₹34.37 CR POWER INFRA ORDERS FROM PGVCL & RVPNL ⚡📈

• 🏆 Rajesh Power Services has secured new orders worth ₹34.37 crore (including taxes) from Paschim Gujarat Vij Company Limited (PGVCL) and Rajasthan Rajya Vidyut Prasaran Nigam Limited (RVPNL) 

• ⚡ From PGVCL, the company received a ₹21.57 crore order for SCADA readiness and rectification of Ring Main Units (RMUs) along with a Comprehensive Maintenance Contract (CMC) across the Bhavnagar, Jamnagar and Rajkot Circles, to be executed in 12 months 

• 🏗️ From RVPNL, it secured a ₹12.80 crore turnkey contract for modification of the existing 220kV Jodhpur–CHB transmission line into an underground cable system, including installation of monopoles, with execution over 9 months 

• 🇮🇳 Both contracts have been awarded by domestic government entities in the normal course of business, with no promoter interest and no related-party transaction involved 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The new government orders strengthen Rajesh Power Services' order book, improve revenue visibility and reinforce its execution capabilities in power distribution and transmission infrastructure.)

SBI: SBI FUNDS MANAGEMENT IPO PROSPECTUS FILED WITH ROC 📑🏦

• 📄 State Bank of India informed that SBI Funds Management Ltd. (SBIFM) has filed its Prospectus dated July 16, 2026 with the Registrar of Companies (ROC), Mumbai, completing another step in its IPO process 

• 💰 The IPO comprises an Offer for Sale (OFS) of 99,501,649 equity shares (4.8851%) by SBI and 71,454,982 equity shares (3.5082%) by Amundi India Holding, subject to finalisation of the Basis of Allotment 

• 💵 The Offer Price has been fixed at ₹574 per share, with an employee discount of ₹54 per share for eligible employees 

• 🗓️ Allotment is expected on or about July 20, 2026, following completion of the book-building process under SEBI ICDR Regulations 

• 📊 For FY26, SBIFM reported total income of ₹4,969.09 crore and reserves & surplus of ₹3,533.09 crore 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The filing of the prospectus marks a key milestone in the successful execution of the SBI Funds Management IPO and supports value unlocking for SBI.)

BHARATROHAN: TO SET UP USA STEP-DOWN SUBSIDIARY VIA NETHERLANDS ARM 🇺🇸🌍

• 🌍 The Board has rescinded its earlier approval dated May 30, 2026 and has freshly approved the incorporation of a step-down subsidiary in the USA through its Netherlands-based wholly owned subsidiary GroeiGids B.V. instead of directly through BharatRohan 

• 🏢 The proposed entity, "BharatRohan INC." (or another approved name), will become a 100% wholly owned subsidiary of GroeiGids B.V. and consequently a step-down subsidiary of BharatRohan Airborne Innovations 

• 🇺🇸 The proposed US entity will operate in the agriculture sector, focusing on marketing, packaging and selling spices, pulses and essential pantry staples to households, retailers and foodservice providers 

• 💰 The initial investment will be made through cash consideration, while the incorporation is still proposed and subject to the necessary approvals in the relevant jurisdiction 

• 🚀 The revised structure aligns the company's international expansion through its existing European subsidiary, supporting its global growth strategy 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The revised overseas structure strengthens BharatRohan's international expansion strategy and establishes a platform to access the US agricultural products market.)

PAYTM , MOBIKWIK: UPI PAYMENTS ABOVE ₹2,000 MAY ATTRACT 0.5% MDR ; FE 

• 🏦 Centre is considering reintroducing Merchant Discount Rate (MDR) of up to 0.5% on UPI transactions above ₹2,000 for large merchants

• 🏪 Businesses with annual turnover up to ₹1.5 crore are likely to remain exempt, irrespective of transaction size

• 📅 A final decision is expected within a month

• 📊 Transactions above ₹2,000 account for only 4% of person-to-merchant UPI transactions; 86% are below ₹500

• 💰 Proposal aims to help banks and payment service providers recover rising operational and technology costs

🟠 Impact: Mixed (Positive for banks and payment companies due to potential fee income; could marginally increase costs for large merchants if implemented.)

**BHEL | UBS Maintains Neutral**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Neutral** rating.
- Target price **₹460**.

**Q1FY27 Performance**
- Revenue grew **40% YoY**.
- EBITDA at **₹500 crore**.
- Revenue beat UBS estimates by **20%**.
- EBITDA beat estimates by **51%**.
- EBITDA margin expanded to **6.5%**.
- Margin exceeded **5.2%** estimate.

**Order Momentum**
- New orders **₹26,700 crore**.
- Driven by large power orders.
- Strong order pipeline maintained.

**Power Business**
- Revenue grew **52% YoY**.
- PBIT margin improved to **9.5%**.
- Execution remained robust.

**Industry Business**
- Orders declined **70% YoY**.
- Revenue increased **12% YoY**.
- PBIT margin moderated to **13.6%**.
- Weak business mix impacted margins.

**Export & Margins**
- Won largest-ever **₹2,300 crore** GTG export order.
- Gross margin expanded **200 bps YoY**.
- Gross margin reached **31.2%**.
- Sequential decline due to mix.

**Key Takeaway**
- **UBS views BHEL's Q1FY27 positively, supported by strong execution, better-than-expected profitability, robust power segment performance and healthy order inflows, while maintaining a Neutral rating as sustained execution and margin delivery remain key to future earnings growth.**

- **While BHEL delivered better execution and margin performance in Q1FY27, JPMorgan believes the market has already priced in the thermal power upcycle and sees limited upside amid slowing order inflows, maintaining an Underperform rating with a ₹220 target price.**

• 💰 The Board has approved a proposal to raise up to ₹1,000 crore through debt instruments, including Tier-II Bonds, Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs), Medium-Term Notes (MTNs), infrastructure bonds and other RBI-permitted securities, in one or more tranches, subject to shareholders' approval

• 📌 August 13, 2026 has been fixed as the Record Date for determining shareholders eligible for the FY26 dividend (if declared) and for e-voting at the AGM 

• 👨‍💼 The Board recommended the appointment of Mahesh Muralidhar Pai as Managing Director & CEO for a 3-year term from October 1, 2026 to September 30, 2029, subject to shareholders' approval 

• 👥 The Board also recommended the reappointment of Lakshmi Ramakrishna Srinivas as Independent Director for a second term of 5 years, and the reappointment of Dolphy Jose as director retiring by rotation, subject to shareholders' approval 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The proposed ₹1,000 crore fundraising enhances capital planning and supports future business growth, while the leadership appointments provide management continuity.)

CITI ON INDIA'S GAS SECTOR

Sees near-term headwinds for India's LNG imports

Asian spot LNG (JKM) prices up 18% to $19.9/MMBtu; LNG slope rises to ~23% of Brent

LNG slope above 15% has historically weighed on India's LNG demand growth

Gas-based power plant utilisation points to softer LNG consumption after the summer peak

Better propane availability and lower global prices may trigger fuel switching in Morbi

Fuel switching could reverse part of the LNG demand surge seen in May-June

Recovery in regasification volumes for Petronet LNG Bed and transmission volumes for GAIL could be impacted

Petronet LNG's stock has shown ~55% negative correlation with LNG slope when it stays above 15%

GAIL could see some offsetting benefits from its gas trading business

IRAN THREATENS SECOND GLOBAL ENERGY CHOKEPOINT

Iran has reportedly told Yemen’s Houthis to be ready to close the Bab el-Mandeb Strait if the U.S. strikes Iranian power infrastructure, according to Reuters sources.

With the Strait of Hormuz already shut, a disruption in the Red Sea would hit the Middle East’s two main oil export routes simultaneously, raising the risk of a severe global energy shock.

Sources say the Houthis have positioned missiles and drones near the Red Sea gateway and are awaiting orders. Saudi Arabia is reportedly treating the threat seriously as tensions between Tehran and Washington continue to escalate.

GE POWER INDIA: APPROVES DEMERGER OF DURGAPUR UNIT TO JSW ENERGY 🔄⚡

• 🏭 Demerger of the Durgapur business unit to JSW Energy to focus on the high-margin services business and exit an underutilized facility

• 🎁 GE Power India shareholders to receive 10 JSW Energy shares for every 139 GE Power India shares, while retaining their existing GE Power India holdings

• 📉 Durgapur facility incurred ~₹27 crore average annual losses, with better utilization expected under JSW Energy

• 📈 Core services order book grew at ~25% CAGR, from ₹299 crore (FY22) to ₹734 crore (FY26)

• 💹 Financial turnaround: Net worth ₹483 crore, EBITDA ₹277 crore, liquidity improved to ₹880 crore, and ICRA upgraded rating to BBB+ (Stable)

• 🤝 Signed a 5-year manufacturing services agreement with JSW Energy to ensure business continuity

🟢 Impact: Positive (Unlocks shareholder value, removes a loss-making asset, sharpens focus on the core business, and provides direct exposure to JSW Energy.)

HAVELLS INDIA: APPROVES ₹255 CRORE CABLE CAPACITY EXPANSION AT TUMAKURU ⚡🏭

• 🏗️ Approved further expansion of cable manufacturing capacity at the Tumakuru, Karnataka facility

• 📈 Capacity to increase from 4,59,600 Kms/year to 7,34,640 Kms/year (~60% expansion)

• 💰 Additional investment of ~₹255 crore, including pending capex from the previous phase

• ✅ Existing expansion phase has already achieved 4,59,600 Kms/year capacity with ₹285 crore invested so far

• 🗓️ Expansion is targeted for completion by December 2027 and will be funded through internal accruals

🟢 Impact: Positive (Capacity expansion strengthens Havells' cable business and supports long-term growth.)

BHEL 

Why is it actually VERY Bullish? 

By reading the raw filing directly, the numbers are nothing short of phenomenal and crush the consensus estimates:

Top-Line Blowout: BHEL posted Revenue from Operations of ₹7,697 Cr, easily beating the ₹6,801 Cr expectation. This represents a massive ~40% YoY growth, shattering the anticipated 20% growth.

Monumental Profitability: Against an estimated Net Profit of just ~₹58 Cr, BHEL delivered a staggering ₹381 Cr in Standalone PAT (~6.5x the consensus).

A Historic Turnaround: Most importantly, the company reported a massive turnaround from a ₹454 Cr loss in the same quarter last year. This quarter officially breaks an eight-year streak of Q1 losses, confirming the highly anticipated turnaround thesis driven by strong order book execution.

**Gravita India | Multi-Metal Recycling Expansion**

**Business Transformation**
- Diversifying beyond lead.
- Building copper platform.
- Expanding value-added portfolio.

**RMIL Acquisition**
- Acquired **~99%** stake in RMIL.
- Acquisition value **₹560 crore**.
- Copper products capacity **31,200 MTPA**.
- Additional capex **₹160 crore**.
- Mundra recycling capacity **29,400 MTPA**.

**Why Copper?**
- EVs need **3x** more copper.
- Demand from renewables.
- Growth in power transmission.
- Data centre demand rising.
- Infrastructure spending supportive.
- Secondary copper CAGR **~14%**.
- Recycled share **55%** by FY30.

**Business Quality**
- Supplies diversified industries.
- Serves defence sector.
- Around **25%** export revenue.
- Utilisation at **40%**.
- Target **65%** by FY28.

**Margin Opportunity**
- Energy use lower **80–85%**.
- Lower production costs.
- Reduced carbon emissions.
- Focus on value-added products.
- Better margins than commodities.

**Growth Outlook**
- Revenue CAGR **38%**.
- EBITDA CAGR **36%**.
- PAT CAGR **27%**.
- Estimates for **FY26–28E**.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Gravita is evolving into India's next multi-metal recycling platform through its strategic entry into copper recycling, positioning itself to benefit from structural demand across EVs, renewables, power infrastructure and data centres while enhancing margins through value-added copper products.**

**NIRMAL BANG ON CEMENT SECTOR**

**Sector View**
- **Outlook:** Positive
- Reinitiates coverage on the sector.

**Key Highlights**
- Structural demand remains strong.
- Demand to outpace capacity additions.
- Housing demand supports growth.
- Government infrastructure spending remains robust.
- Private capex revival adds momentum.
- Positive medium-term outlook.

**Preferred Picks**
- **Ambuja Cements** – **Buy** | **TP: ₹554**
- **Birla Corporation** – **Buy** | **TP: ₹1,234**
- **Dalmia Bharat** – **Buy** | **TP: ₹2,083**
- **JK Lakshmi Cement** – **Buy** | **TP: ₹693**
- **Nuvoco Vistas** – **Buy** | **TP: ₹415**
- **The Ramco Cements** – **Buy** | **TP: ₹1,084**
- **UltraTech Cement** – **Buy** | **TP: ₹13,558**

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nirmal Bang has turned positive on the Indian cement sector, expecting sustained housing demand, infrastructure spending and a recovery in private capex to drive demand growth ahead of capacity additions, supporting a favourable medium-term outlook.**

@Stockupdate9

Recode Studios Acquires 51% Stake in Premium Beauty Brand Aflairza

- Aflairza operates in the premium beauty segment with strong consumer acceptance

- The brand has demonstrated superior value realization, reflecting its premium positioning

- Acquisition aligns with Recode's strategy to expand its presence in the high-growth Beauty & Personal Care category

- Company will also utilize its distribution network and operational expertise to scale the brand

- Founders will continue to partner with Recode in the next phase of expansion

It gives Recode a stronger presence in the premium beauty segment, where higher pricing power and better margins can create long-term value. Leveraging Recode's distribution, product development and execution capabilities could significantly accelerate Aflairza's scale over the coming years.

Recode will initially acquire a 33% stake in Phase I for an aggregate consideration of ₹8.32 Crore, with equal allocation between promoter share purchase & fresh capital into the company.

**JEFFERIES ON NUVOCO VISTAS**

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Buy
- **Target Price:** ₹430

**Key Highlights**
- Strong Q1 EBITDA beat.
- EBITDA up **10% YoY**.
- Better pricing supported earnings.
- Lower petcoke usage reduced costs.
- Better procurement improved margins.
- Execution exceeded expectations.
- Q2 likely to remain seasonally soft.
- Cost inflation impact limited.
- Management guides **<₹50/tonne** cost impact.
- Vadraj commissioning remains key monitorable.
- Ramp-up of acquired asset in focus.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies maintains a Buy rating on Nuvoco Vistas, citing strong pricing-led earnings, effective cost management and successful execution, while the Vadraj plant ramp-up remains the key driver for future growth.**

**NOMURA ON NUVOCO VISTAS**

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Buy
- **Target Price:** ₹400

**Key Highlights**
- Q1FY27 EBITDA beat estimates.
- Higher non-trade realizations supported earnings.
- Q2FY27 expected to remain seasonally weak.
- EBITDA may decline **₹180/tonne QoQ**.
- Cost environment expected stable.
- Capex plans remain unchanged.
- Vadraj expansion progressing on schedule.
- Trades at **8.6x** one-year forward EV/EBITDA.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nomura maintains a Buy rating on Nuvoco Vistas, supported by better realizations, disciplined costs and ongoing capacity expansion, despite expecting a seasonal moderation in profitability during Q2FY27.**

**HSBC ON NUVOCO VISTAS**

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Buy
- **Target Price:** ₹475 *(Raised)*

**Key Highlights**
- Q1FY27 EBITDA beat estimates.
- Higher cement prices drove earnings.
- Q2 seasonally expected to remain weak.
- Better demand expected in H2FY27.
- Pricing environment likely to improve.
- Cost environment turning favourable.
- FY27–FY29 EPS raised **6–11%**.
- Valuation at **5.7x FY28 EV/EBITDA**.
- **US$55/t EV/T** seen attractive.

**Key Takeaway**
- **HSBC remains positive on Nuvoco Vistas, expecting stronger sector fundamentals in H2FY27, supported by improving demand, pricing and costs, while attractive valuations offer further upside.**

text: **STOCK IDEA -** Sky Gold And Diamonds Ltd

**HSBC ON ADANI PORTS & SEZ**

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Buy
- **Target Price:** ₹2,200 *(Raised)*

**Key Highlights**
- Governance risks have eased.
- Deleveraging supports re-rating.
- Better disclosures improve confidence.
- Strong operational compounding continues.
- FY31 driven by organic port growth.
- Container business remains key driver.
- Healthy port economics.
- Market share gains expected.
- Margin expansion likely.
- Logistics business scaling up.
- International ports diversify earnings.
- Marine business boosts ROCE.

**Key Takeaway**
- **HSBC remains bullish on Adani Ports, citing improved governance, stronger balance sheet, robust domestic port growth and expanding logistics and international operations as key drivers of long-term earnings growth and valuation re-rating.**

**HSBC ON PIDILITE INDUSTRIES**

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Buy
- **Target Price:** ₹1,890

**Key Highlights**
- Initiates coverage with Buy.
- Deep distribution network.
- Strong brand portfolio.
- Product innovation advantage.
- Superior execution capabilities.
- Waterproofing drives growth.
- Tile adhesives remain key growth engine.
- Fevicol growing above GDP.
- Strong earnings visibility.
- Attractive long-term growth outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- **HSBC initiates coverage on Pidilite with a Buy rating, highlighting its market leadership, strong distribution, brand strength and multiple structural growth drivers that support sustainable long-term earnings growth.**

**CITI ON DIVI'S LABORATORIES**

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Buy
- **Target Price:** ₹9,450

**Key Highlights**
- Strong GLP-1 growth opportunity.
- Focus on Tirzepatide & Orforglipron.
- Oral peptide pipeline expanding.
- GLP-1 supplies may begin FY27.
- Enlicitide/Icotrokinra from CY27.
- Entresto concerns seen overdone.
- API capacity expansion underway.
- Targets global API leadership.
- Europe LOE impact expected gradual.
- Generics pricing showing stability.
- Strong CY27–30 pipeline visibility.
- Premium valuation backed by growth.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi remains constructive on Divi's Laboratories, citing a strong GLP-1 and peptide pipeline, expanding API capacity and improving generics outlook, with long-term growth visibility supporting its premium valuation.**

**MACQUARIE ON DIXON TECHNOLOGIES**

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Outperform
- **Target Price:** ₹16,000 *(Raised)*

**Key Highlights**
- Vivo JV boosts growth visibility.
- EPS could triple by FY29.
- Revenue CAGR **28%** (FY26–29).
- EPS CAGR **43%** (FY26–29).
- PLI 2.0 offers upside.
- Industrial EMS opportunity.
- Duty cuts could benefit.
- Automotive electronics gaining traction.
- IT hardware remains growth driver.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Macquarie remains highly bullish on Dixon, expecting the Vivo JV and multiple growth levers across EMS, PLI, automotive and IT hardware to drive exceptional earnings growth over the next three years.**

**DAM CAPITAL ON BIOCON**

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Buy
- **Target Price:** ₹495

**Key Highlights**
- Mylan exited entire **5.64%** stake.
- Block deal removes key overhang.
- Some investors may monetize holdings.
- Timing of further sales uncertain.
- Biosimilars business seen improving.
- Momentum expected from H2FY27.
- Scale-up of bUstekinumab.
- Scale-up of bAspart and bDenosumab.
- bAflibercept launch to support growth.
- Revenue CAGR of **15%** expected (FY26–FY28).
- EBITDA margin expected at **26–27%**.

**Key Takeaway**
- **DAM Capital maintains a Buy rating on Biocon, expecting the removal of the Mylan overhang and a stronger biosimilars pipeline to drive meaningful earnings acceleration from H2FY27 onward.**

**BERNSTEIN ON BIOCON**

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Underperform
- **Target Price:** ₹326

**Key Highlights**
- Viatris block deal provides orderly exit.
- No material change to earnings outlook.
- Street optimism seen as excessive.
- High leverage remains a concern.
- Biologics investments yet to scale.
- Syngene growth needs acceleration.
- Policy changes increase competition.
- Biosimilar moat gradually weakening.
- $2 billion revenue target may take 4–5 years.
- Succession strategy remains science-led.
- Commercial execution needs improvement.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Bernstein remains Underperform on Biocon, believing that while the Viatris exit removes an overhang, structural challenges around leverage, competitive intensity and commercial execution continue to limit the company's earnings outlook.**

**CITI ON DIVI'S LABORATORIES**

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Buy
- **Target Price:** ₹9,450

**Key Highlights**
- Strong GLP-1 opportunity pipeline.
- Focus on Tirzepatide and Orforglipron.
- Oral peptide opportunities expanding.
- GLP-1 supplies may begin FY27.
- Enlicitide/Icotrokinra from CY27.
- Entresto concerns seen overdone.
- API capacity expansion underway.
- Targets global API leadership.
- Europe LOE expected gradual.
- Generic pricing stabilising.
- Strong CY27–30 growth visibility.
- Valuation supported by earnings outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi remains positive on Divi's Laboratories, citing robust long-term growth visibility from GLP-1 and peptide APIs, expanding manufacturing capacity and improving generics trends despite premium valuations.**

**KOTAK INSTITUTIONAL EQUITIES ON L&T TECHNOLOGY SERVICES**

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Reduce
- **Target Price:** ₹3,350

**Key Highlights**
- Q1 delivered a reasonable performance.
- Revenue growth slightly ahead.
- Margins improved modestly.
- Plant engineering drove growth.
- Benefiting from strong demand.
- Near-term outlook remains healthy.
- Business mix limits faster growth.
- FY27 revenue growth estimate **5.2% (CC)**.
- FY27 EBIT margin estimate **15.9%**.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Kotak Institutional Equities maintains a Reduce rating on LTTS, believing that while execution and margins remain healthy, its business mix is likely to constrain medium-term growth acceleration.**

**JPMORGAN ON L&T TECHNOLOGY SERVICES**

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Neutral
- **Target Price:** ₹3,300

**Key Highlights**
- Q1 beat on revenue and margins.
- Revenue grew **1.5% QoQ (CC)**.
- Sustainability and Mobility led growth.
- Technology segment remained weak.
- Large deal TCV at **US$100 Mn**.
- Outlook remains optimistic for FY27.
- Growth expected every quarter.
- Deal ramp-ups to support growth.
- Sustainability to lead growth.
- EBITDA margin expanded **50 bps QoQ** to **15.7%**.
- **16.5%** margin target maintained.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JPMorgan remains Neutral on LTTS despite a strong Q1 performance, as improving margins and expected deal ramp-ups are offset by a weaker large-deal pipeline and continued softness in the Technology segment.**

**NOMURA ON L&T TECHNOLOGY SERVICES**

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Neutral
- **Target Price:** ₹3,180

**Key Highlights**
- Q1FY27 delivered decent performance.
- Sustainability vertical led growth.
- EBIT margin **15.7%**.
- Margin beat estimates.
- Margin up **50 bps QoQ**.
- Margin up **200 bps YoY**.
- Deal wins totaled **US$100 Mn**.
- One **US$30 Mn** deal.
- One **US$20 Mn** deal.
- Four deals above **US$10 Mn**.
- Reiterated **Project Lakshya FY31**.
- AI capabilities remain key focus.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nomura maintains a Neutral rating on LTTS, highlighting healthy margin improvement, steady deal wins and continued confidence in its long-term AI-led growth strategy under Project Lakshya FY31.**

**KOTAK INSTITUTIONAL EQUITIES ON TATA ELXSI**

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Sell
- **Target Price:** ₹3,000

**Key Highlights**
- Revenue in line with estimates.
- Profitability disappointed.
- Media & Communications grew.
- Transportation & Medical Devices declined.
- Deal transition costs impacted margins.
- GTM and delivery investments increased costs.
- Cost inflation largely sticky.
- Auto demand remains weak.
- FY27–FY29 EPS cut **10–14%**.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Kotak Institutional Equities maintains a Sell rating on Tata Elxsi, citing persistent margin pressure, weak automotive demand and structurally higher costs that could weigh on earnings over the next few years.**

**JPMORGAN ON TATA ELXSI**

**Rating & Target**
- **Recommendation:** Neutral
- **Target Price:** ₹3,500

**Key Highlights**
- Q1 revenue beat estimates.
- Margins missed sharply.
- Revenue grew **1.3% QoQ (CC)**.
- Media & Telecom led growth.
- Auto and Healthcare remained weak.
- FY27 high single-digit growth maintained.
- Healthcare recovery remains uncertain.
- EU auto business faces headwinds.
- Margin fell **330 bps QoQ**.
- Transition and ramp-up costs impacted.
- Margin recovery expected gradually.
- FY27–FY29 EPS cut by **1–13%**.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JPMorgan remains Neutral on Tata Elxsi as healthy revenue growth was overshadowed by a sharp margin contraction, while recovery depends on Healthcare demand and easing execution-related costs over the coming quarters.**

text:
🇮🇳 Is India losing the global innovation race?

India's innovation lag isn't a govt failure; it's a corporate issue. The country spends just 0.65% of GDP on R&D, vastly below the 2.5-3% global norm for major economies.

Over 60% of R&D is state-funded. Meanwhile, top IT firms returned over ₹6tn to shareholders in the last decade instead of funding tech, choosing short-term margins over long-term R&D.

Tactical play: Pivot away from stagnating giants. Bet on deep-tech startups & niche mid-caps. The entry of foreign players & new competition will act as the catalyst for growth.

 🎯 Bottom Line: Avoid low-growth incumbents returning cash over innovating. Markets will heavily reward companies prioritizing long-term R&D with superior valuation multiples.
